reading.label: B
paragraphs: Every Saturday, a group of *volunteers* meets at a small *community* center. They come to *help* young children learn to read. The *charity* is called Reading Friends. It is a *nonprofit* group, so nobody works for money.

Omar is one of the volunteers. He is a student at the university. He reads easy English books with the children. When a child reads a full page, everyone claps. Last month, the group had a special *event*. They asked people to *donate* old books, and many families brought boxes to the center. Now the small *library* has more than two hundred books.

The children love the new books, and they feel *proud* when they read. Omar feels happy too. He says, 'When we *support* our community, everybody wins.' The volunteers plan to open a second center next year.

questions.question_en: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: A man who buys books at a shop
A big sports event in a city
questions.explanation_ar: النص عن متطوّعين يساعدون الأطفال على القراءة.
قال النص إنهم يجتمعون كل يوم سبت (every Saturday).

questions.question_en: What does 'nonprofit' mean in the passage?
questions.question_ar: ماذا تعني كلمة nonprofit في النص؟
choices: A group that sells many books
A group that does not work for money
A group that helps only adults
questions.correct_answer: A group that does not work for money
questions.explanation_ar: nonprofit تعني مجموعة لا تعمل من أجل المال.

questions.question_en: What did people donate at the special event?
questions.question_ar: بماذا تبرّع الناس في الفعالية الخاصة؟
questions.explanation_ar: طلب المتطوّعون من الناس التبرّع بالكتب القديمة.

choices: Because he gets a lot of money

questions.question_en: How do the children probably feel about the volunteers?
questions.question_ar: كيف يشعر الأطفال تجاه المتطوّعين على الأرجح؟
questions.explanation_ar: الأطفال يحبّون الكتب ويشعرون بالفخر، فمن المرجّح أنهم ممتنّون وسعداء.

questions.question_en: What happened AFTER people donated books?
questions.question_ar: ماذا حدث بعد أن تبرّع الناس بالكتب؟
choices: The library got more than two hundred books
questions.correct_answer: The library got more than two hundred books
questions.explanation_ar: بعد التبرّع، أصبح في المكتبة أكثر من مئتي كتاب.

vocab: a person who works to help and gets no money
The volunteer helps at the center.
all the people who live in one area
He wants to help his community.
a group that helps people for free
The charity gives food to families.
a group that does not work for money
to give something to help others
People donate old books to the center.
The group had a special event.
happy about something good you did
The child feels proud when he reads.
to make something easier for someone
They help children learn to read.
a place with many books to read
The library has many new books.

listening.transcript: Hi, I'm Sara. On weekends I volunteer at a food charity in my city. We're a small nonprofit group. My job is to pack boxes of rice and bread for poor families. Last week, many people came to donate food. I love this work because I help my community, and every family leaves with a big smile.

exercises.explanation_ar: قالت سارة إنها تتطوّع في جمعية خيرية للطعام.
قالت إنها تتطوّع في عطلات نهاية الأسبوع (on weekends).

exercises.question_en: What is Sara's job at the charity?
exercises.explanation_ar: مهمّتها تعبئة صناديق الأرز والخبز للعائلات الفقيرة.

exercises.question_en: What did many people do last week?
exercises.explanation_ar: جاء كثير من الناس ليتبرّعوا بالطعام الأسبوع الماضي.

exercises.question_en: Why does Sara love this work?
options: Because she earns a lot of money
Because it is easy and short
exercises.explanation_ar: قالت إنها تحب العمل لأنها تساعد مجتمعها.

exercises.question_en: What kind of group does Sara work with?
exercises.explanation_ar: وصفت مجموعتها بأنها مجموعة صغيرة غير هادفة للربح (nonprofit).
